title: Promote repaired CUA scrub into intentional multi-screen focus and first-pass left-stick scale
summary: 0.4.0 is the first release that treats the repaired CUA scrub seam as a foundation instead of a one-off rescue. The direct-stick scrub path from 0.3.12 stays in place, but gaze on a different hosted screen now preempts the previous focus owner immediately so scrub and navigation retarget intentionally across multiple independent screen instances. This release also adds the first left-controller prototype: left-stick vertical drives the host CUA's `Control.Scale` through a smooth deterministic tween, with scaling slowed only once the host gets down near 0.1 so tiny screens do not jump around.
reasoning: The user-facing model is deliberately simple: each hosted screen instance stays independent, and gaze chooses which one receives transport input right now. That means the right next step after fixing scrub was not a shared multi-screen manager, but an explicit gaze-preemption rule on the existing static input owner. For scaling, the current host atom already supports a clean `Control.Scale` seam, so the first left-stick prototype should use that instead of reopening authored screen-size changes. The taper below 0.1 keeps very small screens controllable without making normal resizing sluggish.

changes.summary: The repaired direct-stick CUA scrub seam from 0.3.12 is carried forward as the `0.4.0` baseline.
changes.reasoning: This keeps the operator-visible scrub fix while widening the interaction model around it.

changes.summary: When gaze lands on a different hosted screen, that plugin instance now preempts the old shared CUA input owner immediately instead of waiting for release grace to expire.
changes.reasoning: Multiple hosted screen instances already work independently; the missing seam was intentional immediate retargeting of scrub and navigation on gaze.

changes.summary: Left-stick vertical now drives the focused host atom's `Control.Scale` parameter through a smooth deterministic tween.
changes.reasoning: The host atom scale seam already scales the CUA resource cleanly, so it is the safest first resize prototype for left-hand interaction.

changes.summary: Scale speed stays normal above 0.1 and tapers only once the host gets smaller than that threshold.
changes.reasoning: The operator asked for small screens to scale much more slowly without making ordinary resizing feel sluggish.

verification: Compile the CUA player in Release after adding gaze-preemption and left-stick host-scale tweening.
Compile the no-input seam with EnablePlayerInputFeatures=false to bin\ReleaseNoInput\fa_cua_player.dll.
Stamp version 0.4.0 into player.version.json and BuildRuntimeInfo.cs.
Build and deploy the versioned fa_cua_player.0.4.0.dll and fa_player_asset.0.4.0.assetbundle through the existing player foundation script.
Run foundation release validation for 0.4.0 and keep the generated receipt beside the release shelf.
knownIssues: This release intentionally keeps multiple screen instances independent; it does not add any shared playlist, shared state, or cross-instance awareness beyond immediate gaze retargeting.
The new left-stick scale prototype is host-atom scaling only. It does not yet include left-stick depth, lasso movement, or other Fork B manipulation behaviors.
The separate subscene-save and VaM slider serialization behavior is still not part of this fix.
Spatial audio controls remain a separate follow-up seam because the current player runtime still initializes the underlying audio source as 2D.
